new_classes.label_en: Vinegar, fermented vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid (CN 2209)
new_classes.label_de: Speiseessig; Gärungsessig und Essigersatz aus Essigsäure
new_classes.definition_en: Edible vinegar, fermented vinegar, and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, as described under CN heading 2209.

A quality borne by a vinegar that indicates it is produced by acetic fermentation of wine (grape wine), distinguishing it from other vinegars obtained from other fermented alcoholic liquids or from acetic acid.

new_classes.label_en: Wine vinegar, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2209 00 11)
new_classes.label_de: Weinessig, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (KN 2209 00 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine vinegar (vinegar produced by acetic fermentation of wine) presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2209 00 11.

coverage_explanation: The two main discriminating criteria captured are: (1) the vinegar is wine vinegar (produced by acetic fermentation of wine/grape wine), modelled via WineVinegarQuality; and (2) it is presented in containers of ≤ 2 l, modelled via the SmallContainer part restriction. A parent class Vinegar2209 is introduced as no such class existed in the TBox. Coverage is reduced to 0.75 because: (a) the legal note references "additional note 11 to this chapter" and the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209 part (I) second paragraph (1), which likely provide more detailed compositional or production criteria for wine vinegar that cannot be fully formalised without access to those additional notes; (b) the distinction between CN 2209 00 11 and CN 2209 00 19 (its sibling) is entirely determined by container size (≤ 2 l vs > 2 l), which is captured, but finer regulatory scoping from the additional note content is not available in the provided legal text.
